title: MITRE ATT&CK Technique Mapping
description:
For each observed behaviour in Task 1, pick the MITRE ATT&CK technique ID that describes it. One wrong answer disqualifies the set — the board sees the final map with your name on it.

Observed behaviour: The Griot abused a `sudoers.d` entry that allowed `o.adegoke` to run `less` over /var/log/* as root. By opening `less` on an attacker-writable log file and using the `!` shell-escape, they obtained a root shell.

Pick the ATT&CK sub-technique that best describes this.
choices: T1068 — Exploitation for Privilege Escalation (via kernel CVE)
T1548.003 — Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching
T1574.006 — Hijack Execution Flow: Dynamic Linker Hijacking
